long_term_goals: Child will improve expressive language skills.
Child will improve receptive language skills.
Child will improve pragmatic language skills.
short_term_goals: Child will label/identify 26 letters of the alphabet.
Child will take turns in conversation.
session_content.narrative_progress: SLP prompted child to label lowercase letters and match to capital letters. Child independently labeled 18/26 letters and matched 22/26 letters. SLP prompted child assign attributes (which word describes picture) across 20 trials. Child independently answered 13/20 questions; SLP provided prompts to increase accuracy.
session_content.response_to_treatment: Child required cues to increase focus.

documents.document_type: OT Visit Note / Professional Activity Report

clinical_notes.session_summary: Alisha was seen today for OT. Alisha was generally cooperative during today’s session and participated in: (1) Fine Motor Skills (FMS) training using a school-based vocabulary worksheet (copying words five times each, alphabetizing, using words in sentences). Deficits observed: reduced fine motor coordination, decreased handwriting legibility, impaired pacing/task persistence, inconsistent letter formation. Adaptive adjustments were required for writing utensil to improve grasp/control. (2) Cognitive & Social Skills training with structured cognitive-social scenarios (school/home/community). Difficulties noted with abstract reasoning, perspective-taking, flexible problem-solving; maximum verbal cues needed to expand responses/provide logical explanations. Required consistent verbal cueing for posture/body mechanics, pacing, safety awareness; attention variable and required redirection.

outcomes_and_notes.ifsp_outcome_1: Aaron will use his eye and hand to coordination to improve his grasping skills so that he can manipulate toys, crayons and spoon in order to gain independence to perform daily activities.
outcomes_and_notes.session_summary: Aaron was observed matching and stacking shapes on shape sorter with gestural. Aaron pointed at the shapes to request them with gestural. Aaron pretended eating chocolate and strawberry cake during cooking activity. Aaron was able to put fruits on the birthday cake. Aaron matched, pushed and pulled sheep by color with gestural. Aaron imitated a sheep’s sound with gestural. Aaron followed one step instruction during session.
outcomes_and_notes.between_visits_plan: Parent agreed to engage Aaron in matching shapes on busy book with gestural. Aaron will pointed at the shapes to request them with gestural.
developmental_steps: Improve ability to engage in visual motor activities such as 9 piece large puzzles with wrist.
Color by grasping crayon between thumb and pad of index finger and crayon resting on middle finger.
Imitating lines when handwriting, and spoon feeding.
Improve sensory challenges to respond to instructions.
Rotate to fit into respected spacing, using one hand to stabilize puzzle while other hand places piece.
